properties.remark: HOMES AND STREETS FLOODED IN TOWN. INITIAL REPORT FROM EL PASO COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE.
PEAK GUST REPORTED AT SAN AUGUSTIN PASS
PEAK GUST AT HACHITA VALLEY RAWS
POWER POLE DOWN ON GATO ROAD IN UPPER VALLEY
MEASURED WIND GUST OF 58 MPH AT DIANA AND HONDO PASS.
WIND GUST OF 62 MPH NEAR RUSHING AND TM ROAD.
WIND GUST OF 61 MPH AT CHAPIN HIGH SCHOOL.
WIND GUST OF 52 KTS OR 60 MPH AT THE EL PASO INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT.
COCORAHS OBSERVER
REPORTED BY WSMR METEOROLOGIST AT INTERSECTION OF ROADRUNNER PKWY AND MISSION RD.
ROADS FLOODING AT ZARAGOZA AND ROJAS
EL PASO TIMES REPORTED 20 FAMILIES FLOODED OUT WITH ONE HOME LOST DUE TO FLOODING IN AREA OF COKER ROAD. CARS ALSO WASHED AWAY.
HURLEY STORM DRAINAGE DITCH 8 FEET WIDE BY 5 FEET HIGH OVERFLOWING AND SPILLING ONTO ADJACENT STREET.
STRONG GUSTS EST 50-60 MPH RAISING WIND AND DIRT.
WIND DAMAGE TO HANGAR AT AIR PARK.
RESIDENT REPORTS SIGNIFICANT FLOODING ALONG THE CREEK AND ADJACENT PROPERTIES AND ACROSS LOW WATER CROSSINGS.
VERY HEAVY RAIN IN TOWN...AND STRONG RUNOFF FROM RAIN WEST OF TOWN HAS NUMEROUS BUILDINGS AND STREETS FLOODED ACROSS TOWN.

VERY HEAVY RAIN OVER TOWN HAS FLOODED MANY BUILDINGS AND STREETS ACROSS TOWN. NEW MEXICO STATE UNIV. RECORDED 3.36 INCHES OF RAIN. AT LEAST 6 BUILDINGS WITH WATER DAMAGE...AND SEVERAL ROADS CLOSED. SEVERAL WATER RESCUES IN STALLED VEHICLES ON CAMPUS.

AT LEAST 4 LIGHT POLES BLOWN DOWN BY HIGH WINDS NEAR MILE MARKER 49 ON INTERSTATE 10 4 ENE OF FABENS. EXTENSIVE HAIL WAS COVERING THE GOURND WITH .49 INCHES OF RAIN MEASURE IN 20 MINUTES AT FABENS ELEMENTARY SCHOOL.
GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL REPORTED AT INTERSECTION OF I-10 AND GEORGE DEITER
1 FOOT OF MOVING WATER OBSERVED ACROSS INTERSECTION OF TRANS MOUNTAIN AND RUSHING ROADS
66 MPH WIND GUST FROM WHITE SANDS MESONET OBS SITE.
ONE INCH HAIL REPORTED BY ASOS OBSERVER AT EL PASO INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT.
PING PONG BALL SIZED HAIL REPORTED AT A BORDER PATROL CHECKPOINT ON HIGHWAY US54. SMALLER HAIL COVERING THE GROUND.
REPORT RELAYED FROM WSMR MET TEAM...SAMS MESONET.
